title: Helictochloa bromoides
taxonomy: Helictochloa bromoides is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is also known as bitter bluegrass or meadow brome. The classification of Helictochloa bromoides is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Liliopsida, the order Poales, the family Poaceae, the genus Helictochloa, and the species H. bromoides. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Poaceae family. Helictochloa bromoides is often compared to the related species Bromus bromoides.

intro: Helictochloa bromoides, also known as Brome or False Brome, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its feathery inflorescence and narrow leaf blades. Traditionally, Helictochloa bromoides has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Helictochloa bromoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Helictochloa bromoides is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and to promote wound healing.
